skill.name: singlecell-portal
skill.description: Programmatically query public single-cell study metadata from the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal REST API when you need to search and filter datasets by organism, tissue, disease, or cell type without an API key.
skill.summary: Query public single-cell study metadata from the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal by organism, tissue, disease, or cell type.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The singlecell-portal skill is a legitimate API client for the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal. Of 49 static findings, 22 external_commands findings in SKILL.md are false positives caused by markdown code block backticks misinterpreted as Ruby shell execution. The network findings are true positives reflecting the core function of querying a public REST API. The main confirmed concern is verify=False in scripts/query.py (lines 57, 151, 172), which disables SSL certificate verification for Windows compatibility. No credential access, data exfiltration, or obfuscation was found. The skill is safe to publish with a LOW risk level.

medium_findings.title: SSL Certificate Verification Disabled
medium_findings.description: All three HTTP GET requests in scripts/query.py use verify=False, disabling SSL certificate validation. This exposes the connection to man-in-the-middle attacks. While documented as a Windows compatibility workaround, this weakens transport security and should only be used when necessary.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: verify=False is explicitly set on three separate requests. The pattern is unambiguous and confirmed by the code context. This is a real security weakness, though documented as a deliberate Windows compatibility choice.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Base URL
low_findings.description: The API base URL is hardcoded as a string constant in scripts/query.py. While it points to the legitimate Broad Institute endpoint and the skill has no user-configurable URL injection, hardcoded URLs reduce flexibility and create a single point of failure if the API endpoint changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API_BASE constant at line 21 is a clear hardcoded URL. This is benign in context since it targets an official public API endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: SSL Verification Disabled on All HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: All requests.get() calls across three functions use verify=False, disabling SSL certificate chain validation. This pattern appears in get_facets_dict (line 57), search_by_facets (line 151), and search_by_keyword (line 172).
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All three requests.get calls explicitly pass verify=False. The code imports urllib3 and disables SSL warnings at line 44, confirming this is an intentional pattern.

content.user_title: Query Single Cell Portal for Research Studies
content.value_statement: Researchers need to find relevant single-cell studies without manually browsing portal websites. This skill provides programmatic access to the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal REST API to search and filter studies by organism, tissue, disease, or cell type using ontology-based IDs.
actual_capabilities: Search for public single-cell studies filtered by organism (human, mouse), tissue (lung, brain, liver), disease (cancer, normal), and cell type
Retrieve study metadata including accession numbers, cell counts, descriptions, and study names
Fetch facet dictionaries to discover all valid filter values before constructing queries
Get detailed information about individual studies using their accession number
Automatic conversion of common biological terms to ontology IDs for precise filtering
Fall back to keyword-based search when ontology IDs cannot be determined
limitations: Returns only study metadata, not raw sequencing data or expression matrices
Limited to public studies indexed in the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal
Requires internet access to query the remote API
Does not support authentication-protected endpoints or private studies

use_cases.title: Literature Review and Study Discovery
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a literature review on lung biology can quickly find all relevant single-cell studies indexed in the portal.

use_cases.title: Meta-Analysis Data Curation
use_cases.description: A bioinformatician building a meta-analysis pipeline can programmatically collect study metadata before downloading raw data for analysis.

use_cases.title: Educational Resource Development
use_cases.description: An instructor preparing teaching materials on single-cell genomics can discover example datasets for classroom exercises and demonstrations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Search by Organism and Tissue
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for single-cell studies for human lung tissue.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple two-facet query that returns study names and accession numbers for human lung studies.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Facet Filtered Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find cancer studies for human brain tissue and return at least 5 results.
prompt_templates.scenario: Combining multiple filters (organism and disease) for a targeted literature search.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Available Filter Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me what filter categories and values are available for searching studies in the Single Cell Portal.
prompt_templates.scenario: Exploring the API facet dictionary to understand valid search parameters before constructing queries.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword Fallback Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for studies related to T cell in the Single Cell Portal.
prompt_templates.scenario: Using keyword search when ontology-based filtering does not match or the exact term is unknown.

output_examples.input: Search for single-cell studies for human lung tissue.
output: Found 5 studies matching human lung tissue:
- Human Lung Cell Atlas | accession: SCP123 | cells: 250,000
- COVID-19 Lung scRNA-seq | accession: SCP456 | cells: 150,000
- Idiopathic Pulmonary Fibrosis | accession: SCP789 | cells: 85,000
Available filter categories: organism, tissue, disease, cell_type

best_practices: Use ontology-based facet filtering instead of keyword search for more precise and reproducible results.
Inspect available facet values first before constructing queries to ensure filter terms are valid.
Always use defensive dict.get() access on study objects since response structure may evolve over time.
anti_patterns: Disabling SSL verification in production environments without understanding the security implications of man-in-the-middle exposure.
Assuming all returned studies contain the same metadata fields without defensive null checks.
Using keyword search exclusively when ontology-based facet filtering would yield more targeted results.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The Single Cell Portal API endpoints are public and do not require any authentication.

faq.question: What data can I retrieve with this skill?
faq.answer: Study metadata including name, accession number, cell count, and description. Raw expression data is not included.

faq.question: How do I search by multiple criteria?
faq.answer: You can combine organism, tissue, disease, and cell type filters. The skill converts common terms to ontology IDs.

faq.question: Why does the script disable SSL verification?
faq.answer: The verify=False setting addresses Windows certificate issues. Enable verify=True when possible for better security.

faq.question: Can I query private or restricted studies?
faq.answer: No. Only public studies in the Broad Institute Single Cell Portal are accessible through this API.

faq.question: What happens if my search term is not recognized?
faq.answer: The skill falls back to keyword-based search when no ontology ID can be found for the given term.
